name: Derbyshire Oatcakes (English)
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the oatmeal, flour, and salt in a warm bowl.
2. Cream the yeast with the sugar and add 1/2 pint of the warm water.
3. Pour the yeast mixture into the dry ingredients and gradually add the rest of the water.
4. Mix slowly until a thin batter is formed.
5. Set aside in a warm place until well risen, about 30 minutes.
6. Grease a large frying pan and heat.
7. Pour cupfuls of the batter onto the hot pan and cook like thick pancakes for 4-5 minutes on each side.
8. The oatcakes will keep for 2-3 days. Serve warmed up in a frying pan with bacon and eggs or with lemon juice and sugar, or toasted with cheese or golden syrup.
9. To make a small quantity, mix the flour, oatmeal, and salt with water to form a thin batter. Add the baking powder just before cooking.
